quote_text: The telc German B1-B2 Nursing demonstrates language competence in reading, listening, writing and speaking at level B1 and B2.
The content and nature of the texts reflect the everyday working life of nurses.

International nursing staff must demonstrate language skills at the advanced B2 level in order to work in Germany. The Goethe-Test PRO Pflege is recognised by the German Foreign Office as a B2 language certificate.

Yes, the Goethe-Test PRO Pflege is listed as a B2 language certificate by the following official bodies: Federal Office for Migration and Refugees (BAMF) ... Federal Government portal for skilled workers abroad: Make it in Germany.
The overall result B2 is achieved when all four parts of the examination are passed at least at level B2.
German language skills: at least level B2 of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFR)
depending on the federal state where the training will take place, the applicant needs German language skills at level B2 or B1.
On average, there are only 44 jobseekers for every 100 registered qualified nursing jobs.
A total of 35,000 vacancies for workers in the nursing sector were registered on average in 2023.
Of the total number of qualified nurses, 270,000 employees (16 per cent) are foreign nationals.
The proportion of foreign workers in nursing professions is now 18 percent.
Berufsfeldübergreifende Kurse mit Zertifikatsabschluss (400 bis 500 UE) vermitteln Ihren Mitarbeitenden einen international anerkannten Sprachstand für den Beruf (Niveaustufen A2 bis C2).
Vorbereitungskurse auf Fachsprachprüfungen (100 bis 600 UE) unterstützen Ihre Mitarbeitenden bei der Anerkennung ausländischer Berufsabschlüsse, z.B. im Gesundheitswesen.

As part of the Triple Win project, nursing and care specialists from India (Kerala and Telangana), Indonesia, the Philippines and Tunisia can be recruited. The foundation for this recruitment are placement agreements.
GIZ organises German courses (up to level B1) and specialist nursing courses for the future nursing staff. On request, the courses can also be organised up to level B2.
Hand in Hand for International Talents recruits qualified professionals with vocational training in Brazil, India and Vietnam.
The Triple Win programme recruits trained specialists from India (Kerala and Telangana), Indonesia, the Philippines and Tunisia, as well as nursing trainees from India (Kerala).
THAMM Plus encourages creating paths of safe and fair labour migration to Germany for trainees and skilled workers from Egypt, Morocco, and Tunisia.
